title: Core Network Outage — Glasgow MSC Cluster

affected_services.name: Circuit-Switched Voice — Glasgow MSC Pool
affected_services.description: All CS voice calls originating or terminating in Glasgow MSC area failing. Calls receive network busy or fast busy tone.

affected_services.name: SMS Delivery — Glasgow SMSC Route
affected_services.description: SMS delivery failing for all subscribers homed on Glasgow MSC. Messages queuing at SMSC.

affected_services.name: Mobile Data — Glasgow S-GW Cluster
affected_services.description: Data sessions experiencing intermittent drops and high latency. S-GW failover to Edinburgh partially absorbing load.

affected_services.name: VoLTE — Glasgow IMS Core
affected_services.description: VoLTE registration failing. SIP 503 responses from P-CSCF. Affected subscribers unable to establish IMS sessions.

affected_services.name: Enterprise OneNet — Glasgow Region
affected_services.description: Enterprise OneNet users in Glasgow region unable to make or receive calls. SIP trunking to corporate PBXs down.

root_cause_hypothesis: Dual power supply failure at Glasgow Centralised Core (GCC) site following UPS battery string failure during scheduled mains transfer. Both MSC-S blades in Glasgow pool lost power simultaneously. Standby generator start-up sequence failed due to ATS (Automatic Transfer Switch) firmware fault.

timeline.event: Mains power transfer initiated at GCC site for scheduled utility maintenance
UPS battery string A reported anomalous voltage drop — 48V bus falling below 42V threshold
Mass alarm storm — 487 NodeB/eNodeB sites reporting S1/Iu-CS link failures to Glasgow MSC pool
NOC primary monitoring dashboard showing Glasgow MSC-S01 and MSC-S02 unreachable
NOC Tier 1 operator acknowledged alarm cluster and created incident VF-INC-2026-00847
Automatic escalation to Tier 2 — Core Network Operations triggered by alarm correlation engine
Tier 2 engineer confirmed total MSC pool loss. Customer-facing impact validated via probe data.
Major Incident declared. MIM bridge VF-MIM-2026-00847 opened. P1 SLA clock started.
MIM bridge joined by: Core Network Engineering, Power & Facilities, NOC Shift Lead, Service Management
GCC site facilities team reported UPS battery string A failure. Generator ATS failed to engage. Manual generator start-up initiated.
Standby diesel generator started manually. AC mains power restored to GCC core equipment room.
MSC-S01 beginning boot sequence. Estimated 15-20 minutes to full service restoration.
Ofcom MERITS notification prepared — threshold of 100,000 subscribers exceeded.
MSC-S01 online and accepting registrations. MSC-S02 in boot sequence. CS voice partially restored.
MSC-S02 online. Full MSC pool restored. Subscriber re-registration surge in progress.
All services confirmed restored. Monitoring shows normal KPIs. MIM bridge stood down to monitoring mode.

escalation_history.reason: Automated alarm correlation — MSC pool loss
MSC infrastructure failure requiring specialist engineering
Customer-facing outage exceeding 100,000 subscribers — Major Incident threshold

site.name: Glasgow Centralised Core (GCC)
site.address: Unit 7, Skypark, 8 Elliot Place, Glasgow G3 8EP

notes: Root cause confirmed as UPS battery string A degradation combined with ATS firmware bug (known vendor issue, patch available but not yet applied). Post-incident actions: (1) emergency ATS firmware upgrade across all core sites, (2) UPS battery load testing programme to be accelerated, (3) Glasgow GCC UPS battery string A replacement ordered — ETA 48 hours. Ofcom MERITS report submitted within 24 hours per regulatory obligation.
